subject: [HW-R0R2.0A.0Z.04] There are LAN SCAN COMPARE error during Reboot cycle test
issue_description: During the reboot cycle test, there were 69 instances of LAN SCAN COMPARE errors. The NVIDIA CX-7 device on LAN port 10 disappeared and could not be detected after the error.
log_snippet: LAN SCAN Compare Test: 69/100. Device at BUS 10:00.0/1, NVIDIA CX-7, disappeared. Dmesg reported fatal hardware error, PCIe error, and machine check exception. SEL recorded critical interrupt and fatal processor error.
comments: Severity was raised to Critical and component to System. Additional inspection found the cable connected to root port 10 was bent excessively, and round 32 also hit a CX-7 HW error.
current_config: Configuration A: SKY-621V4 MB A101-1, Intel Xeon 6781P, BIOS S621V406000X006, BMC 1.03, BL 0.40, BMCCONF 0.05, NVIDIA L40S x4, Intel X710-TL x1, Mellanox CX-7 x2, Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS.
expected_config: No LAN SCAN COMPARE errors should occur during reboot testing.
known_context: DQA-created bug with structured test case, fail rate, detailed logs, procedure, and test configuration.
reproduction: Auto Reliability Test - System Reboot Test. Failure observed during reboot cycle with LAN/PCIe SCAN COMPARE review.
test_case: Auto Reliability Test - System Reboot Test
test_procedure: 1. Power on the system and enter the BIOS. 2. Run the OS reboot cycle test. 3. At the end of the test, observe the PCIe SCAN COMPARE error.
evidence: Severity changed from Low/Cosmetically to Critical
NVIDIA CX-7 on LAN port 10 disappeared
Fatal hardware error and machine check exception appear in logs
SEL shows critical interrupt and fatal processor error
